chief_complaint: A 41-year-old male patient suddenly experienced a cerebral infarction.
history: The patient has lower limb osteosarcoma and was diagnosed with Trousseau syndrome, which includes clinical manifestations such as pulmonary embolism, arterial thrombosis, deep vein thrombosis, and nonbacterial thrombotic endocarditis.
exam: The patient was conscious, spoke fluently, and had grade IV muscle strength in the left upper limb. The left lower limb had limited movement because of the tumor.
labs: Emergency cerebral angiography, mechanical thrombectomy, and percutaneous superselective arteriography were performed.
correct_diagnosis: Lower limb juvenile sarcoma combined with Trousseau syndrome, cerebral infarction
differentials: Lower limb juvenile sarcoma combined with Trousseau syndrome, cerebral infarction
correct_ranking: Lower limb juvenile sarcoma combined with Trousseau syndrome, cerebral infarction
